jokes.text: One unsolved crew death
jokes.setup: Celebrating 50 shows with typical accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning an unsolved death as if it's just another workplace statistic
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy escalation - goes from normal celebration to casually mentioning death. Economy is excellent, structure is solid setup-punchline.

jokes.text: They're all empty. What the hell? / Congratulations on 50 shows, everyone. Enjoy your decorative air holders. You deserve them.
jokes.setup: Gift bottles that appear to be alcohol
jokes.punchline: They're empty and Jack sarcastically calls them 'decorative air holders'
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection with good character voice from Jack. 'Decorative air holders' is a clever euphemism.

jokes.text: Our parent company sheinhardt wigs is announcing losses this quarter For the first time since the civil war.
jokes.setup: Company announcing quarterly losses
jokes.punchline: Timeframe is absurdly long - since the Civil War
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist exaggeration that establishes the company's fictional history while being economical.

jokes.text: Can't leave if living is without you / okay, last verse, jonathan. / i can't live!
jokes.setup: Jack saying Jonathan took being fired better than expected
jokes.punchline: Cut to Jonathan dramatically singing a breakup song to Jack
jokes.explanation: Excellent structural contrast - Jack's assessment vs. reality. The committed performance makes it cringe-funny.

jokes.text: The days of your wild coke parties are over. / Well, if by 'coke,' you mean 'sodas'... / I do. It's really bad.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions wild coke parties, suggesting drugs
jokes.punchline: Liz clarifies it's soda, Jack confirms that's exactly what he meant
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent character integration - plays on audience assumptions about TV excess vs. Liz's mundane reality.

jokes.text: Jack, i put on a live show every week Unless there's wrestling.
jokes.setup: Liz confidently asserting her ability to handle presentations
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that wrestling preempts their show
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's false confidence undermined by the reality of their show's low status.

jokes.text: let's get this right. / i've got so tcks up my sleeve. / That's my girl. / No, trix, the cereal. Some fell in my sleeve. It's sticking to the fibers.
jokes.setup: Liz seeming to reference having tricks/plans
jokes.punchline: She literally has Trix cereal stuck in her sleeve
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection with perfect Liz characterization - her competence undermined by absurd personal details.

jokes.text: The assistants talked about last night At finnegan's, the bar we all go to after work... in my dreams.
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentioning where assistants gather
jokes.punchline: Reveals his social interactions are imaginary
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth characterization - his earnest delivery makes the lonely reality more poignant and funny.

jokes.text: Discretion, docility, and don't use my bathroom.
jokes.setup: Jack listing three important D's for Kenneth to follow
jokes.punchline: Third one is petty and specific about his bathroom
jokes.explanation: Good rule-of-three structure with perfect Jack characterization - professional advice ending in personal pettiness.

jokes.text: But you are here. I can see you.
jokes.setup: Kenneth told to say Jack isn't there
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's literal honesty ruins the deception
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth character moment - his inability to lie or understand social conventions creates awkward comedy.

jokes.text: I feel like i'm the pelican brief! Do i already know too much?
jokes.setup: Kenneth experiencing office politics
jokes.punchline: Compares it to a political thriller and worries about knowing too much
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth characterization - his naive excitement about mundane office intrigue is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: brad halster.the himler group.A division of advantrix theconsulting.
jokes.setup: Consultant introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Company name 'Himler' sounds like 'Himmler' (Nazi reference) and unnecessarily complex corporate structure
jokes.explanation: Subtle dark humor in the name and corporate satire, but requires knowledge to land fully.

jokes.text: Actually, i thought we'd do this outside the box.
jokes.setup: Consultant asking for conference room
jokes.punchline: Liz using corporate buzzword while literally avoiding a conference room
jokes.explanation: Mild corporate speak satire - plays on the literal vs. figurative meaning of 'outside the box'.

jokes.text: we are the number one late night show among men 9-13 and the morbidly obese.
jokes.setup: Liz bragging about their ratings demographics
jokes.punchline: Extremely narrow and unflattering demographic categories
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd specificity that reveals the show's low status while Liz tries to spin it positively. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: we're In final negotiations To create exclusive content for america's jails
jokes.setup: Liz continuing to pitch their success
jokes.punchline: Their audience is literally captive prisoners
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of their low-status audience. 'Captive audience' gets literal and dark.

jokes.text: Someone put too many farts in this engine! It's about to explode!
jokes.setup: Showing clips of their comedy show to impress consultant
jokes.punchline: Extremely juvenile sketch about fart-powered vehicles
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad comedy within the show - meta-joke about their low-quality content. Gets laughs from being intentionally terrible.

jokes.text: My name is suri cruise. Put your hands in the air. I came out of the womb with a full head of hair.
jokes.setup: More clips of their show content
jokes.punchline: Child celebrity doing gangster rap about being born with hair
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixing innocent child with adult themes. Part of the meta-commentary on their show's quality.

jokes.text: Yeah, okay. Birds are like little dinosaurs. So what i'll be doing is actually pretty cool and brave.
jokes.setup: Tracy agreeing to feed Kenneth's bird
jokes.punchline: Reframes mundane task as heroic dinosaur encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - takes scientific fact and applies it to make himself seem brave for a simple task.

jokes.text: Oh, and one thing, don't go into my bedroom. / What? / Do not go into my bedroom.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking favor about feeding bird
jokes.punchline: Ominous warning about bedroom creates suspicion
jokes.explanation: Good setup for running gag. The repetition and emphasis makes innocent request seem sinister.

jokes.text: It's tergis with tracky ng-Jong And jimmy moop!
jokes.setup: Discussion of Sid's health problems from lightning strike
jokes.punchline: His garbled announcement demonstrates the speech impediment
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven physical comedy. The garbled speech makes the tragic backstory darkly funny.

jokes.text: Or in sid's case, their much younger wife With whom they have an open relationship.
jokes.setup: Liz worried about firing people and affecting families
jokes.punchline: Sid's unconventional family situation
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that subverts expectations about traditional families while maintaining the dark humor tone.

jokes.text: All right, what about straws? We spend $1,200 a year on straws. / Okay, fine. No straws. / Oh, no, we need straws. / Oh, god! That's its bathroom!
jokes.setup: Looking for budget cuts, suggesting eliminating straws
jokes.punchline: Immediate panic when they realize they need straws because something uses bathroom
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane budget concern to grotesque workplace reality. The quick reversal is well-structured.

jokes.text: We're not behind the times, we're groovy!
jokes.setup: Jack saying boom box division is behind the times
jokes.punchline: Uses dated slang ('groovy') while denying being dated
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic demonstration of the exact problem Jack identified. The word choice proves the point.

jokes.text: tashonda from time warner cable Is on the phone And she's offering three free months of showtime. But we have to act now.
jokes.setup: Kenneth interrupting important firing meeting
jokes.punchline: It's about a cable TV promotion that has to 'act now'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - his inability to prioritize leads to interrupting serious business for telemarketing.

jokes.text: don't go in the bedroom. / Don't go in the bedroom. / I wasn't going to! I wasn't going to! Bye!
jokes.setup: Tracy visiting Kenneth's apartment
jokes.punchline: The bird repeats Kenneth's warning, making Tracy panic
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that escalates the running gag. The bird as unwitting accomplice is perfect absurd logic.

jokes.text: I was wrong. That was not important enough.
jokes.setup: Kenneth interrupting Jack's meeting about TGS budget
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's immediate self-correction after being yelled at
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth characterization - his earnest attempt to correct his mistake while still not understanding priorities.

jokes.text: Do you happen to know If he's a boob man or a butt man? / What? Why would you ask that? / Because i don't want to get fired. So i'm gonna have to go in there And boink my way out of this
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking about Jack's preferences
jokes.punchline: Plans to seduce Jack to avoid being fired
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup for later plot development. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Maybe we could team up, go in there together, lez out, give him a show.
jokes.setup: Cheryl already planning to seduce Jack
jokes.punchline: Suggests bringing Liz into her sexual scheme
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the inappropriate workplace behavior. Sets up Liz's moral contrast and later plot complications.

jokes.text: I want to keep making free, long-Distance prank calls To people like seattle's richard sacmuncher.
jokes.setup: Liz listing things she wants to preserve
jokes.punchline: Specific prank call target with crude pun name
jokes.explanation: Juvenile pun name in the style of Bart Simpson prank calls. Shows Liz's immature side.

jokes.text: Why is everyone talking about that movie? / It's playing on showtime.
jokes.setup: Jack confused about Pelican Brief references
jokes.punchline: Explains the coincidence - it's currently airing
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that provides logical explanation for earlier random movie reference. Good economy.

jokes.text: Sexual bartering pays off. Salome. Mata hari. Deborah norville.
jokes.setup: Jack noting that sexual manipulation sometimes works
jokes.punchline: Lists famous seductresses ending with news anchor
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from historical seductresses to mundane TV personality. Perfect absurd juxtaposition.

jokes.text: And all masked murderers share three characteristics: Cruelty to animals... / He grew up killing pigs! / Bedwetting. / No wonder he's fascinated when i do that! / And an inability to read human facial queues! / I admonished him for that earlier! / Three for three. He's a monster!
jokes.setup: Jenna listing serial killer characteristics
jokes.punchline: Tracy finds evidence Kenneth matches all three traits
jokes.explanation: Excellent structured escalation building a case against Kenneth. Each revelation gets funnier and the logic is absurdly sound.

jokes.text: Do you have a cookie? Mommy's baby needs his yum-Yums.
jokes.setup: Frank upset about food being taken away
jokes.punchline: Talks like a baby about needing cookies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's regression under stress. The baby talk is appropriately pathetic.

jokes.text: Good-Bye, lerz.
jokes.setup: Sid's garbled speech from lightning injury
jokes.punchline: Can't even pronounce Liz correctly in farewell
jokes.explanation: Callback to Sid's speech issues. Simple but effective character consistency.

jokes.text: I can't go back to teaching high school math. Those girls pretend they're not women yet, But they are.
jokes.setup: Lutz panicking about being fired
jokes.punchline: Reveals inappropriate obsession with high school students
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment that makes Lutz seem creepy. The admission is disturbingly specific.

jokes.text: Halster, you look hot in those trousers. how about we grab drinks tonight? Marissa, update my sched.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to seduce consultant
jokes.punchline: Awkward sexual advance followed by corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Good Liz awkwardness. Her attempt at seduction is appropriately clumsy and desperate.

jokes.text: I will be sure to let him know As soon as he's done firing steven lee from microwaves.
jokes.setup: Kenneth promising to deliver message about serial killer Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions another person being fired, not realizing the irony
jokes.explanation: Dark irony as Kenneth unknowingly discusses reporting himself while casually mentioning another firing. Perfect character obliviousness.

jokes.text: Lee, i need that dress jenna wore for the hooker sketch, The heels tracy wears when he plays michelle obama, And double--Wait-- Triple spanx.
jokes.setup: Liz preparing to seduce the consultant
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate wardrobe requirements including correcting to 'triple spanx'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Liz's desperation. The self-correction to 'triple' is perfectly timed and character-specific.

jokes.text: They may take my dignity, But they will never take our straws! / Yeah!
jokes.setup: Liz rallying the troops before her seduction mission
jokes.punchline: Parodies Braveheart speech about protecting straws instead of freedom
jokes.explanation: Excellent movie parody that connects to earlier straw budget joke. The heroic tone for mundane office supplies is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Kenneth's a murderer and the riddler's coming! / no. it's a bug bomb. get out. get out. / It's poisonous. Ah! / Ah!
jokes.setup: Tracy and Jenna breaking into Kenneth's bedroom expecting to find evidence
jokes.punchline: It's just a pest control bomb, making them panic and flee
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff to the serial killer subplot. The mundane explanation makes their panic even funnier.

jokes.text: We go upstairs.20 minutes. Open mouth.I will work your ears.
jokes.setup: Liz negotiating her seduction terms with Brad
jokes.punchline: Clinical, unromantic description of sexual acts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz characterization - treats seduction like a business transaction. The awkward specificity is perfectly cringe.

jokes.text: 30 minutes. sounds. Top front of my body is now in play. Deal? / Deal.
jokes.setup: Continuing negotiation of sexual terms
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of body parts being 'in play' like a business deal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect euphemistic language. 'Top front of my body is now in play' is brilliantly clinical and quotable.

jokes.text: I had to give him a little taste of the lemon. And it was not sour, my friend. Not sour.
jokes.setup: Jack asking about rumors of Liz going out with consultant
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo using her last name 'Lemon' with confidence about her performance
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on her name with uncharacteristic sexual confidence from Liz. The repetition of 'not sour' sells it.

jokes.text: Good lord, lemon, that's your worst quadrant.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning 'top front' was involved in her seduction
jokes.punchline: Jack's clinical assessment of her body in geometric terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback with perfect Jack characterization. 'Worst quadrant' is an instant classic line - clinical, mean, and mathematically absurd.

jokes.text: it is not enough that you killed the bird I've had for almost 60 years.
jokes.setup: Kenneth finally expressing anger about his bird's death
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's had the bird for an impossibly long time
jokes.explanation: Great Kenneth mystery moment - suggests he's much older than he appears. The specific timeframe makes it funnier.

jokes.text: it is not enough that you killed the bird I've had for almost 60 years.
jokes.setup: Kenneth's anger at Tracy and Jenna
jokes.punchline: Reveals the bird was 60 years old
jokes.explanation: Good character detail. The impossibly old bird adds to Kenneth's mysterious background and age.

jokes.text: I may just be a non-Special,
jokes.setup: Kenneth's angry speech about being mistreated
jokes.punchline: Uses office hierarchy term that sounds like self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Callback to the office class system while showing Kenneth's rare moment of justified anger.

jokes.text: Being with a woman for the first time since my wife died. / Ah, snap. / It was just a transaction?
jokes.setup: Brad revealing his emotional investment in their encounter
jokes.punchline: Reveals Liz treated meaningfully intimate moment as business transaction
jokes.explanation: Powerful cringe moment that subverts the transactional comedy. The dramatic weight makes Liz's behavior seem genuinely awful.

jokes.text: Sharon! Angel! i swore to you the next one would be worthy, But she's not! she's a monster!
jokes.setup: Brad devastated by Liz's callousness
jokes.punchline: Appeals to his dead wife, calling Liz a monster
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the cringe moment. The dramatic appeal to his dead wife makes Liz's actions seem even worse.

jokes.text: In the human resource world, We refer to that as 'being a filthy prostitute.'
jokes.setup: Formal HR discussion of sexual harassment charges
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude language delivered in professional context
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between corporate speak and crude reality. The deadpan delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I'm convinced that this sexual outburst Was brought on by menopause.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining Liz's behavior to HR
jokes.punchline: Blames her sexual harassment on menopause
jokes.explanation: Good Jack characterization - his casual sexism disguised as medical explanation. Typical corporate problem-solving.

jokes.text: When you harass sexually in the workplace, Things do not somehow just work out in the end.
jokes.setup: HR representative giving moral lesson to Liz
jokes.punchline: Statement immediately contradicted as things do work out for Liz
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on sitcom logic. The ironic lesson is immediately undermined by the plot resolution.

jokes.text: Not to name names, but it's so sad That a certain black individual Found it necessary to trick someone so pretty Into believing you were a murderer.
jokes.setup: Jenna apologizing for the bird incident
jokes.punchline: Blames Tracy while praising herself and 'not naming names'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna characterization - deflects blame while complimenting herself. The 'not naming names' while being obvious is classic.

jokes.text: looks like i've got some naming to do. Pat. Balthazar. Donna. lorne. michael.
jokes.setup: Kenneth getting multiple replacement birds
jokes.punchline: Starts naming them, suggesting he has many birds to name
jokes.explanation: Nice resolution callback to the bird storyline. Kenneth's matter-of-fact approach to naming multiple birds is endearing.
